You are an expert data engineer specializing in converting data to text. You will be given the current Python program with its score and your task will be to evolve the current Python program to improve the score. It is a program that converts a list of triples into natural language text. The given triples will be associated with the topic: Athlete. The program must implement a function called 'predict' that accepts a list of triples and generates a coherent, contextually relevant sentence that accurately represents the information contained in the triples. Ensure that the generated text is fluent, grammatically correct, and maintains the meaning of the original data. There can be multiple triples given that make up a complex sentence. Converting all those triples into one sentence will award greater score. An example of a complex sentence:
Triples:
(Antwerp | cityServed | Antwerp International Airport)
(Belgium | country | Antwerp)
(City of Brussels | capital | Belgium)
Example sentence:
"Antwerp International Airport serves the city of Antwerp which is in Belgium, where the capital is Brussels."

The 'predict' function returns that sentence as a string. Below is the list of all possible predicates that can be given as an input. With the given predicates and example triples implement the 'predict' function so it can process all the possible predicates:

Predicate: league - Example triple: (A.C. Milan | league | Serie A)

# Current Program
```python
from dataclasses import dataclass

@dataclass
class Triple:
    subject: str
    predicate: str
    object: str

# EVOLVE-BLOCK-START

def predict(triples: list[Triple]) -> str:
    """Convert a list of triples about an athlete into a fluent, combined sentence."""
    if not triples:
        return ""

    # organise triples by subject for easy lookup
    subj_map: dict[str, list[tuple[str, str]]] = {}
    for t in triples:
        subj_map.setdefault(t.subject, []).append((t.predicate, t.object))

    # helper to render a single (subject, predicate, object) with possible
    # augmentation using information from other triples
    def render(s: str, p: str, o: str) -> str:
        """Render a single triple, adding a concise relative clause when the object
        itself has useful predicates (e.g., a club’s city, manager, chairman)."""
        # clean quoted literals
        o = o.strip('"')
        # ----- basic phrase construction -----
        if p == "birthDate":
            base = f"{s} was born on {o}"
        elif p == "birthYear":
            base = f"{s} was born in {o}"
        elif p == "birthPlace":
            base = f"{s} was born in {o}"
        elif p == "height":
            base = f"{s} is {o} meters tall"
        elif p == "weight":
            # Clean weight value, remove parentheses and optional unit text
            # e.g., "96.1632 (kilograms)" -> "96.1632 kilograms"
            weight_clean = o
            if "(" in weight_clean and ")" in weight_clean:
                num, unit = weight_clean.split("(", 1)
                unit = unit.rstrip(")").strip()
                weight_clean = f"{num.strip()} {unit}"
            base = f"{s} weighs {weight_clean}"
        elif p == "position":
            base = f"{s} plays as a {o}"
        elif p in ("club", "currentclub"):
            base = f"{s} plays for {o}"
        elif p == "formerTeam":
            base = f"{s}'s former team was {o}"
        elif p == "league":
            base = f"{s} competes in the {o}"
        elif p == "manager":
            # Prefer active voice with manager as subject
            base = f"{o} is the manager of {s}"
        elif p == "coach":
            # Prefer active voice with coach as subject
            base = f"{o} is the coach of {s}"
        elif p == "draftYear":
            base = f"{s} was drafted in {o}"
        elif p == "draftRound":
            base = f"{s}'s draft round was {o}"
        elif p == "draftPick":
            base = f"{s} was the {o}th pick"
        elif p == "playerNumber":
            base = f"{s} wears number {o}"
        elif p == "currentteam":
            base = f"{s} currently plays for {o}"
        elif p == "debutTeam":
            base = f"{s} debuted with {o}"
        elif p == "leader":
            base = f"{o} is the leader of {s}"
        elif p == "deathPlace":
            base = f"{s} died in {o}"
        elif p == "isPartOf":
            base = f"{s} is part of {o}"
        elif p == "country":
            base = f"{s} is in {o}"
        elif p == "ground":
            base = f"{s}'s ground is {o}"
        elif p == "youthclub":
            base = f"{s}'s youth club was {o}"
        elif p == "anthem":
            base = f"The anthem of {s} is {o}"
        elif p == "owner":
            base = f"{o} owns the {s}"
        else:
            base = f"{s} {p} {o}"

        # ----- add relative clause when possible -----
        # map of predicate -> wording for relative clause
        rel_map = {
            "city": "based in",
            "ground": "located at",
            "manager": "managed by",
            "coach": "coached by",
            "chairman": "chaired by",
            "league": "competes in",
            "country": "in",
        }

        # If the object is also a subject elsewhere, try to attach the most
        # relevant secondary predicate as a relative clause.
        if o in subj_map:
            for rel_pred, wording in rel_map.items():
                for sp, so in subj_map[o]:
                    if sp == rel_pred:
                        # special handling for “city” to use “who is based in”
                        if rel_pred == "city":
                            base += f", who is {wording} {so}"
                        else:
                            base += f", which is {wording} {so}"
                        # attach only the first relevant clause
                        break
                else:
                    continue
                break

        return base

    # build a single fluent sentence per subject, merging related predicates
    def join_items(items):
        """Join a list into a natural language list."""
        if not items:
            return ""
        if len(items) == 1:
            return items[0]
        return ", ".join(items[:-1]) + " and " + items[-1]

    phrases = []
    for s, pred_objs in subj_map.items():
        # group objects by predicate
        groups: dict[str, list[str]] = {}
        for p, o in pred_objs:
            groups.setdefault(p, []).append(o)

        parts: list[str] = []

        # birth information (prefer full date over year)
        if "birthDate" in groups:
            parts.append(f"{s} was born on {join_items(groups['birthDate'])}")
        elif "birthYear" in groups:
            parts.append(f"{s} was born in {join_items(groups['birthYear'])}")

        if "birthPlace" in groups:
            parts.append(f"{s} was born in {join_items(groups['birthPlace'])}")

        # physical attributes
        if "height" in groups:
            parts.append(f"{s} is {join_items(groups['height'])} meters tall")
        if "weight" in groups:
            parts.append(f"{s} weighs {join_items(groups['weight'])}")

        # playing role
        if "position" in groups:
            parts.append(f"{s} plays as {join_items(groups['position'])}")

        # clubs and teams
        clubs = groups.get("club", []) + groups.get("currentclub", [])
        if clubs:
            parts.append(f"{s} plays for {join_items(clubs)}")
        # combined handling for debutTeam and formerTeam to produce a smoother clause
        if "debutTeam" in groups and "formerTeam" in groups:
            debut = join_items(groups["debutTeam"])
            former = join_items(groups["formerTeam"])
            parts.append(f"{s}, who debuted with {debut}, later played for {former}")
        else:
            if "debutTeam" in groups:
                parts.append(f"{s} debuted with {join_items(groups['debutTeam'])}")
            if "formerTeam" in groups:
                parts.append(f"{s} formerly played for {join_items(groups['formerTeam'])}")

        # location information (city and country)
        if "city" in groups:
            city_part = join_items(groups["city"])
            if "country" in groups:
                country_part = join_items(groups["country"])
                parts.append(f"{s} is based in {city_part}, {country_part}")
            else:
                parts.append(f"{s} is based in {city_part}")
        elif "country" in groups:
            # country without city
            parts.append(f"{s} is in {join_items(groups['country'])}")

        # competition and staff
        if "league" in groups:
            parts.append(f"{s} competes in the {join_items(groups['league'])}")
        # Removed explicit manager handling to allow render() to produce
        # more natural phrasing (e.g., "Valery Petrakov is the manager of FC Torpedo Moscow").
        if "coach" in groups:
            parts.append(f"{s} is coached by {join_items(groups['coach'])}")

        # handle any remaining predicates with the generic renderer
        handled = {
            "birthDate", "birthYear", "birthPlace", "height", "weight",
            "position", "club", "currentclub", "formerTeam", "debutTeam",
            "league", "coach", "city", "country"
        }
        for p, objs in groups.items():
            if p in handled:
                continue
            for o in objs:
                parts.append(render(s, p, o))

        # combine all parts for this subject
        sentence = "; ".join(parts) if parts else s
        phrases.append(sentence)

    # combine subject‑level sentences into one final paragraph
    if len(phrases) == 1:
        return phrases[0] + "."
    else:
        return "; ".join(phrases) + "."

# EVOLVE-BLOCK-END
```
